ucs.title: [LLM] APT36 Crimson RAT loader chain — Office macro spawns script host that drops .NET dropper beaconing on non-standard high TCP port
ucs.description: Hunts Transparent Tribe's (APT36 / Mythic Leopard / COPPER FIELDSTONE) signature Crimson RAT delivery chain: a maldoc impersonating Indian MoD / DRDO / Indian Army spawns wscript/mshta/cmd/powershell from Office, which drops a .NET binary into a user-writable directory, and that binary then beacons over raw TCP to a public IP on a non-standard high port (8000-65535) — the canonical CrimsonRAT C2 fingerprint Kaspersky / Talos / Cisco have repeatedly attributed to this actor.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as childSpawn from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wordview.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_id Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as dropTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as dropped_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.scr" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port values(All_Traffic.app) as c2_app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.transport=tcp AND All_Traffic.dest_port>=8000 AND All_Traffic.dest_port<=65535 AND NOT All_Traffic.dest_port IN (8080,8443,8888,9090,3389,5985,5986) AND All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_name | rename All_Traffic.src as dest All_Traffic.process_name as net_process] | where dropTime>=childSpawn AND dropTime<=childSpawn+300 | table _time dest user parent_process_name process_name process dropped_path dropped_hash c2_ip c2_port net_process
ucs.kql:
let CorrelationWindow = 10m;
let OfficeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wordview.exe"]);
let ScriptHosts = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let WriteablePaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\"]);
// Stage 1 — Office spawns script host (T1204.002 + T1059.005)
let ScriptSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeApps)
    | where FileName in~ (ScriptHosts)
    | project SpawnTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              OfficeApp = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              OfficeCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ScriptHost = FileName,
              ScriptCmd  = ProcessCommandLine;
// Stage 2 — that activity drops a binary into a user-writable path (T1027.013)
let Dropped = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where FolderPath has_any (WriteablePaths)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ScriptHosts) or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeApps)
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
              DroppedPath = FolderPath, DroppedFile = FileName,
              DroppedSha256 = SHA256;
// Stage 3 — egress on high TCP port to public IP — Crimson RAT C2 fingerprint
let Beacon = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","ConnectionAttempt")
    | where Protocol == "Tcp"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemotePort between (8000 .. 65535)
    | where RemotePort !in (8080, 8443, 8888, 9090, 3389, 5985, 5986)
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl,
              C2Process = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              C2ProcessSha256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              C2FolderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath;
ScriptSpawn
| join kind=inner Dropped on DeviceId
| where DropTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + CorrelationWindow)
| join kind=inner Beacon on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
// require the beaconing process to live in the same writeable path tree as the drop
| where C2FolderPath has_any (WriteablePaths)
| project SpawnTime, DropTime, NetTime, DeviceName, AccountName,
          OfficeApp, ScriptHost, ScriptCmd,
          DroppedPath = strcat(DroppedPath, DroppedFile), DroppedSha256,
          C2Process, C2FolderPath, C2ProcessSha256, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let CorrelationWindow = 10m;
let OfficeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wordview.exe"]);
let ScriptHosts = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let WriteablePaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\"]);
let ScriptSpawn = ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
             ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ParentLeaf in (OfficeApps)
    | where ChildLeaf  in (ScriptHosts)
    | project SpawnTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              OfficeApp = ParentLeaf, ScriptHost = ChildLeaf,
              ScriptCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine;
let Dropped = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | extend ActLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".exe" or TargetFileName endswith ".scr" or TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (WriteablePaths)
    | where ActLeaf in (ScriptHosts) or ActLeaf in (OfficeApps)
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              DroppedFile = TargetFileName, DroppedPath = TargetFilePath,
              DroppedSha256 = TargetFileSHA256;
let Beacon = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where NetworkProtocol =~ "Tcp"
    | where DstPortNumber between (8000 .. 65535)
    | where DstPortNumber !in (8080, 8443, 8888, 9090, 3389, 5985, 5986)
    | where not(ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr))
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              RemoteIP = DstIpAddr, RemotePort = DstPortNumber,
              C2Process = ActingProcessName;
ScriptSpawn
| join kind=inner Dropped on DvcHostname
| where DropTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + CorrelationWindow)
| join kind=inner Beacon on DvcHostname
| where NetTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
| project SpawnTime, DropTime, NetTime, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          OfficeApp, ScriptHost, ScriptCmd,
          DroppedPath, DroppedSha256, C2Process, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.rationale: This is APT36's bread-and-butter loader pattern, not a generic Office-macro detection. Three actor-specific anchors are stacked: (1) the Office-document → script-host transition that Talos / Kaspersky / Zscaler have published in every Crimson-RAT report since 2016, (2) drop-path and extension matching the .NET Crimson RAT/Capra RAT staging artefacts (Roaming\<random>.exe), and (3) a raw TCP beacon

ucs.title: [LLM] APT36 Indian-government lookalike domain in inbound email — typosquatted gov.in/nic.in/mod.gov.in delivery
ucs.description: Hunts Transparent Tribe's preferred initial-access pretext (T1583.001 + T1566.001/.002): inbound email with attachment or URL referencing a domain that mimics India's Ministry of Defence, Indian Army, DRDO, ISRO, MHA, or other gov.in/nic.in subdomains, but is not on the legitimate suffix. APT36 has been documented using domains like governmentofindia[.]in, indianarmy-nic[.]in, drdo-nic[.]in, mod-gov[.]in, defenceforumindia[.]com.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Email.subject) as subjects values(All_Email.file_name) as attachments values(All_Email.url) as urls from datamodel=Email.All_Email where All_Email.message_info="inbound" AND All_Email.action="delivered" by All_Email.src_user All_Email.recipient All_Email.message_id | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | eval combined=mvappend(subjects, urls, attachments) | mvexpand combined | rex field=combined "(?i)(?<lookup_domain>(?:[a-z0-9-]+\.)+[a-z]{2,})" | eval dl=lower(lookup_domain) | where match(dl, "(?i)(gov|nic|mod|mil|drdo|isro|mha|army|navy|airforce|defence|defense|cisf|crpf|bsf|raw|cbi|afspa|ssb|incometax|niti|rbi|sebi|indianembassy|moefcc|mea|mospi)") AND NOT match(dl, "(?i)(\.gov\.in$|\.nic\.in$|\.gov\.pk$|\.gov\.bd$|microsoft\.com$|google\.com$|cloudflare\.com$)") | stats count min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(subjects) as subjects values(src_user) as senders values(recipient) as recipients by dl message_id | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | rename dl as suspect_domain
ucs.kql:
let GovKeywords = dynamic(["gov","nic","mod","mil","drdo","isro","mha","army","navy","airforce","defence","defense","cisf","crpf","bsf","raw","cbi","afspa","ssb","incometax","niti","rbi","sebi","indianembassy","moefcc","mea","mospi"]);
let IndianLures = dynamic(["posting","transfer","promotion","defence","army","jawan","officer","ministry","circular","order","AFSPA","DGMO","DRDO","ISRO","appointment","pension","medal","NDA","siachen","kashmir","LOC"]);
let LegitGovSuffix = dynamic([".gov.in",".nic.in",".gov.pk",".gov.bd",".gov.np",".gov.lk"]);
let SuspectMail = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
    | where DeliveryAction in ("Delivered","DeliveredAsSpam")
    | extend SenderDomLower = tolower(SenderFromDomain),
             SubjectLower  = tolower(Subject)
    | where SenderDomLower has_any (GovKeywords)
         or SubjectLower has_any (IndianLures)
    | project Timestamp, NetworkMessageId, SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain,
              SenderIPv4, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, AttachmentCount, UrlCount;
// pull every URL associated with these messages and extract candidate gov-themed domains
SuspectMail
| join kind=inner (
    EmailUrlInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | project NetworkMessageId, Url, UrlDomain
  ) on NetworkMessageId
| extend UrlDomLower = tolower(UrlDomain)
| extend GovTheme = (UrlDomLower has_any (GovKeywords)) or (tolower(SenderFromDomain) has_any (GovKeywords))
| where GovTheme
| where not(UrlDomLower endswith ".gov.in" or UrlDomLower endswith ".nic.in" or UrlDomLower endswith ".gov.pk")
| where not(tolower(SenderFromDomain) endswith ".gov.in" or tolower(SenderFromDomain) endswith ".nic.in")
| where not(UrlDomLower in~ ("microsoft.com","google.com","cloudflare.com","office.com"))
// optional: enrich with Safe-Links click — high-fidelity escalation
| join kind=leftouter (
    UrlClickEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","ClickedThrough")
    | project NetworkMessageId, ClickTime = Timestamp, ClickerUpn = AccountUpn, IsClickedThrough
  ) on NetworkMessageId
| project Timestamp, ClickTime, IsClickedThrough,
          SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain, SenderIPv4,
          RecipientEmailAddress, ClickerUpn,
          Subject, SuspectDomain = UrlDomLower, Url
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let GovKeywords = dynamic(["gov","nic","mod","mil","drdo","isro","mha","army","navy","airforce","defence","defense","cisf","crpf","bsf","raw","cbi","afspa","ssb","incometax","niti","rbi","sebi","indianembassy","moefcc","mea","mospi"]);
let IndianLures = dynamic(["posting","transfer","promotion","defence","army","jawan","officer","ministry","circular","order","afspa","dgmo","drdo","isro","appointment","pension","medal","nda","siachen","kashmir","loc"]);
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where OfficeWorkload =~ "Exchange"
| where Operation in~ ("Deliver","Receive","MailItemsAccessed")
| extend SenderLower = tolower(tostring(Sender)),
         SubjectLower = tolower(tostring(Subject))
| extend SenderDom = tolower(tostring(split(SenderLower, "@")[1]))
| where SenderDom has_any (GovKeywords) or SubjectLower has_any (IndianLures)
| where not(SenderDom endswith ".gov.in" or SenderDom endswith ".nic.in" or SenderDom endswith ".gov.pk")
| where not(SenderDom in~ ("microsoft.com","google.com","cloudflare.com","office.com"))
| project TimeGenerated, MessageId, InternetMessageId, Sender, SenderDom,
          Recipients, Subject, ClientIP, OriginatingServer
// fold in proxy hits that resolve a gov-themed lookalike domain inside the same window
| union (
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | extend HostLower = tolower(coalesce(DestinationHostName, RequestURL))
    | where HostLower has_any (GovKeywords)
    | where not(HostLower has_any (".gov.in",".nic.in",".gov.pk","microsoft.com","google.com"))
    | project TimeGenerated, SourceIP, SourceUserName, RequestURL, DestinationHostName,
              DeviceVendor, DeviceProduct
  )
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Pakistan-attributed APT36 operationally targets Indian defence, paramilitary, and diplomatic personnel almost exclusively, and CrowdStrike (MYTHIC LEOPARD) / SEQRITE / Talos / Volexity have repeatedly documented their use of typosquatted Indian-government domains (e.g. governmentofindia[.]in, indianarmy-nic[.]in, drdo-nic[.]in, mod-gov[.]in) and lure subjects matching military / MoD / DRDO adminis
